_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create different types of texts — written and spoken — with well-developed, organised ideas for a specific purpose and audience, including multimodal elements.

_source.w: We are learning to create different types of texts — written and spoken — with well-developed, organised ideas for a specific purpose and audience, including multimodal elements.
y: Creating purposeful, audience-aware texts with developed ideas shows mature writing ability.
Including multimodal elements enhances our communication and reflects how texts work in the real world.
This versatility as writers prepares us for the demands of secondary school and beyond.
eg: We write a well-structured argument with developed reasoning and evidence for a specific audience.
We create an information text with organised ideas, technical vocabulary and supporting visuals.
We write a narrative with a complex plot, developed characters and a distinctive voice.
We deliver a spoken presentation that combines content, structure and visual elements effectively.
We adapt our writing style and content for different purposes and audiences.
We peer-review each other's texts against criteria for purpose, audience and quality.
